data.title: VCX Fund Soars 1,000% in 5 Days as SpaceX IPO Hype Nears Fever Pitch — DXYZ on the Rise
data.description:
Shares in the Fundrise Innovation Fund (VCX) climbed over $340 per share early Wednesday, marking about a 991% jump from its opening price of $31.25 five days ago. The closed-end fund has continued to surge as more retail investors chase pre-IPO (initial public offering) exposure to heavyweight private companies on Wall Street, with recent headlines from Elon Musk's SpaceX likely adding more fuel to the fire.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade TSLA with leverage
  • New trading tool for TSLA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

According to reports, the space exploration company could file its IPO prospectus as early as this week or the next, with SpaceX expected to raise more than $75 billion from its debut at a valuation of $1.25 trillion. In addition to this, Musk has recently stated plans to pump about $25 billion into building a new chip-manufacturing facility for Tesla (TSLA), SpaceX, and xAI.

Apart from VCX, the Destiny Tech 100 (DXYZ), which was launched in 2022 and debuted on the New York Stock Exchange in 2024, is now also attracting attention. DXYZ was up about 18% to $31.39 per share as of 6:34 a.m. EST, adding to its 9.27% gain from the previous day.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: China Cracks Down on Food Delivery Price Wars, Lifting Alibaba (BABA), JD.com (JD), and Meituan Stocks
data.description:
China tech stocks moved higher after regulators signaled a shift in how food delivery firms compete, and that could be a quiet win for Alibaba Group Holding (BABA). Shares of Meituan (MPNGF) jumped as much as 13% in Hong Kong, while Alibaba gained about 6.7% and JD.com (JD) rose 5.7%. The move came after China's market regulator held a meeting to curb unfair competition across the sector.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, a state-backed Economic Daily column called for an end to the ongoing price wars. The report said the industry has fallen into a "vicious cycle" of losing money just to gain attention, which in turn weighs on consumer recovery.

Meanwhile, BABA shares dropped 0.46% on the NYSE on Tuesday, closing at $125.48.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: SpaceX vs. Anthropic: VCX and DXYZ Funds Offer Indirect Exposure to Private AI/Space Tech
data.description:
In the competitive world of private tech, SpaceX and Anthropic lead their fields. SpaceX dominates space travel with reusable rockets, Starlink's global internet constellation, and frequent NASA contracts. Anthropic advances AI safety through models like Claude, backed by heavyweights such as Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOGL).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Direct investment in these private companies remains unavailable to most retail investors due to their non-public status. However, two publicly traded funds: the Fundrise Innovation Fund, LLC (VCX) and Destiny Tech100 Inc. (DXYZ) offer indirect access to these unicorns. Below, we examine their holdings, performance, and potential.

VCX's Rapid Rise Driven By Anthropic Exposure

Fundrise Innovation Fund is a closed-end interval fund, which has surged nearly 900% since its recent listing. The fund trades at a significant premium to its net asset value (NAV) of $18.26 per share as of early 2026, as per Fundrise disclosures. This premium reflects strong investor demand, though it introduces volatility as hype fades.

VCX's portfolio boasts a hefty 20.7% allocation to Anthropic, a standout amid the AI boom. The fund focuses on late-stage ventures in transformative tech, with quarterly redemption windows providing some liquidity.

DXYZ: A Diversified Bet on SpaceX and Anthropic

Destiny Tech100 (DXYZ), another closed-end fund, holds substantial SpaceX stakes and added $100 million in Anthropic exposure via a special purpose vehicle (SPV) on February 11, 2026. This brings its combined weighting in SpaceX and Anthropic to around 20%, mirroring VCX's emphasis.

DXYZ once traded far above NAV but now hovers just dollars over it, with 17% short interest suggesting rebound potential. It offers stock-like liquidity for otherwise illiquid private assets. DXYZ currently holds 27 companies, but targets 100 in total, spanning AI, space, biotech, and more.

Why a Dual SpaceX + Anthropic Bet Is Better

SpaceX, valued at around $350 billion, boasts tangible milestones: over 100 launches last year and multi-billion-dollar government deals. Anthropic, at around $61.5 billion, competes fiercely with OpenAI in safe AI development.

Funds like VCX and DXYZ democratize access to these "locked-up" assets. DXYZ's broader Tech100 basket adds diversification, while VCX's Anthropic focus has fueled its gains, potentially lifting DXYZ via shared sentiment.

Investing in these funds also poses risks such as premium/discount volatility, trading well above or below NAV, alongside illiquidity from private holdings that limit quick exits. VCX, for instance, imposes quarterly redemption caps of 5-25%. Tech valuations also swing wildly (DXYZ plunged over 80% in 2025), compounded by high management fees of 2-3%. Even so, for fans of AI/space, these funds are a smart proxy showdown.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: New Netgear Trade Idea After FCC Ban Drives a Boost
data.description:
It happened overnight. The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) announced a ban on the sale of new foreign-made consumer router models. 

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

More than 60% of routers sold in the U.S. are estimated to have been manufactured in mainland China. According to the federal agency, cyberattacks on small businesses and households in the U.S. have been on a rapid rise since 2024. The FCC feels that this is at least partially due to national security concerns posed by foreign-made routers. Routers that are already here and have already been approved are still eligible for sale in the U.S. This applies to new routers.

Netgear (NTGR) is an interesting case. The firm is headquartered in the U.S., and its equipment is designed in the U.S. However, that gear is manufactured elsewhere and would be included in the ban. Netgear, some expect, will be granted a conditional approval as a U.S. company by either the Department of Defense/War or Department of Homeland Security. NTGR stock is up more than 10% in the past day.

Could Use the Help

Netgear could use the help. The firm posted adjusted earnings and revenue beats for its fiscal fourth quarter. Those results were released in early February. There was no sales growth, however. For the current quarter, which is due to be reported in late April, the few analysts that actually cover this name are looking for an adjusted EPS of -$0.08 on revenue of $158.7 million. That number would be good for a year-over-year contraction of roughly 2%.

Interestingly, on this news, analyst Tore Svanberg of Stifel Nicolaus, who is rated at five stars (out of five) by TipRanks, opined on the stock. Svanberg reiterated both his "Buy" rating and his $36 target price. The stock is not heavily followed on Wall Street.

The Chart​
data.author: TheStreet Pro

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/25/26 – Stock Futures Rise on U.S.-Iran Peace Negotiations
data.description:
U.S. stock futures climbed early Wednesday after President Trump announced that the U.S. had sent a 15-point peace plan to Iran. Oil prices fell sharply due to rising hopes for de-escalation. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 1.05%, 0.89%, and 0.93%, respectively, at 5:27 a.m. EST on March 25.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was down 1.01% at $98.92 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) fell 1.05% to $87.90 per barrel.

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower, reversing earlier gains from President Trump's claims of "productive conversations." Markets dipped after Iranian officials denied direct peace talks with the U.S. The Dow fell 0.18%, the Nasdaq declined 0.84%, and the S&P 500 shed 0.37%.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield fell to near 4.33%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price hovered around $4,565 per ounce on Wednesday.

No key economic reports are scheduled today. On the earnings front, companies reporting today include Beyond Meat (BYND), Chewy (CHWY), PDD Holdings (PDD), Paychex (PAYX), and Jefferies (JEF).

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today amid signs of U.S. efforts to de-escalate tensions.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets advanced on March 25, as global investors reacted to easing U.S.-Iran tensions.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 1.09%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 1.30%, while the Shenzhen Component surged 1.99%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei increased 2.87% and the Topix added 2.57%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Gain on Hopes of Progress Toward Middle East Peace
data.description:
U.S. stock futures inched higher on Tuesday evening as optimism grew over potential de‑escalation in the Middle East following reports that the U.S. has proposed a peace plan to Iran. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.79%, 0.63%, and 0.61%, respectively, at 6:34 p.m. EDT on March 24.

  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Major stock indices declined in the regular trading session as oil prices climbed again. Also, markets pulled back after Iranian officials denied holding direct peace talks with the U.S., reversing gains driven by President Trump's earlier claims of "productive conversations." The Nasdaq, the Dow, and the S&P 500 dropped 0.8%, 0.2%, and 0.4%, respectively.

On the earnings front, Chewy (CHWY) and Paychex (PAYX) are scheduled to report quarterly numbers tomorrow.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Meta Platforms Boosts Top Executives' Pay with Stock Options amid AI Push
data.description:
Social media giant Meta Platforms (META) is offering its top executives stock options in an effort to retain talent as the AI race heats up. This is the first time that the company has offered its top executives stock options since its initial public offering (IPO) in 2012. META stock was up 1.1% in Wednesday's pre-market trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta Offers Stock Options to Incentivize Key Talent

Meta Platforms' decision to offer stock options comes as the company is trying to gain an edge in generative AI and compete more effectively by retaining key AI talent.

According to SEC (Securities and Exchange Commission) filings, CFO Susan Li, chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth, chief product officer Chris Cox, COO Javier Olivan, president Dina Powell McCormick, and chief legal officer Curtis Mahoney ​are eligible for the stock options. Interestingly, CEO Mark Zuckerberg is not getting any options.

In a statement, a Meta spokesperson called the move a "big bet." He added that these "pay packages will not be realized unless Meta achieves massive future success," and the share price significantly exceeds the exercise price. The first tranche of options will be paid when META stock hits $1,116.08, an 88% jump from Tuesday's closing price of $592.92. The next tranche requires a stock price of $1,393.87, while the highest tranche targets a price of $3,727.12.

With OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google-parent Alphabet (GOOGL) aggressively launching AI models and tools, Meta Platforms is trying to remain competitive in the AI race. The company announced a capex in the range of $115 billion to $135 billion for 2026 to support its AI push. Meta's capital spending forecast for 2026 marks a significant jump from the $72.2 billion spent last year.

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Meta Platforms stock based on 40 Buys and five Holds. The average META stock price target of $865.58 indicates 46% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: 8 Stocks and ETFs to Buy in this Risky Market, According to Top Analysts
data.description:
Our team at TheStreet Pro is made up of a group of sophisticated traders, investors, and analysts. They don't think like most people. In fact, they're always asking questions, trying to stay one step ahead of the market. And that's no easy feat these days. AI offers the potential to change our world, while the war in Iran offers more immediate and no less dramatic change.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade NVDA with leverage
  • New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

This week, I went to three of our best (OK, they're all our best) analysts to find out what questions they're asking as they seek to provide the answers our subscribers need to invest successfully.

Peter Tchir Has Questions About the War in Iran

Peter Tchir is required reading, especially these days. When he's not writing for TheStreet Pro, his day job is Head of Macro Strategy at Academy Securities. His coworkers include retired generals and military intelligence officers. When he speaks about the war, I listen.

How's Peter feeling these days? Cautious. On Monday, he wrote that after covering hedges just before the market rocketed higher, he added them back again. You can read more about it in Trump Prompts Volatility With Shallow Attempt to Smooth Markets Amid Iran Conflict.

Why is Peter cautious? Because he's asking the right questions and isn't sure what the answers are.

First, Trump's 5-day pause in the fighting leads him to wonder if he's using these 5 days to just get troops in order for an attack over the weekend. After all, the administration has a pattern of taking action on weekends and then soothing the markets during the week. 

Second, he's wondering about the impact on oil prices in the U.S. Many people point out that in the U.S., we don't need outside oil right now. But the oil market is global, and the prices that we pay are still set by global supply and demand. With supply choked, prices are up.

Third, he wonders about the economy. Interest rates are rising, inflation is on the move, and jobs data is softening. Consumers are likely to pull back in this environment.

Fourth, he wonders how the increase in energy prices will impact AI and spending on new data centers. After all, AI is a huge consumer of energy.

Last, Peter wonders about the supply chain. As we saw when the Texas power grid went out in 2021, supply chain interruptions can take time to filter through the system and even cause inflation. What part of today's supply chain has been impacted enough that we'll be feeling it down the road?

Peter doesn't have answers for these questions. Nobody does. But asking those questions helps to figure out where to put your money.

So, the big question is, where is Peter investing these days? Bonds. He feels that we're nearing the point where one of two scenarios will play out: Oil will fall in price, or people will start talking about a recession. Both of those scenarios are good for bonds.

Specifically, Peter is buying TLT, the iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (TLT).

Louis Llanes Has Questions About the Strait of Hormuz and Asia

I've known Louis Llanes longer than any other contributor at TheStreet Pro. He's one of those people who has the experience to understand human psychology and the ability to use a computer to find the best ideas to take advantage of human behavior.

While investors are worried about what is going wrong, Louis asks, what could go right?

What could go right? Well, right now, people are worried about the Strait of Hormuz. It's a choke point in the Middle East waterway by which a significant percentage of the world's oil flows. And on Monday, when Louis wrote Strait of Hormuz Reopening? These Asia ETFs Could Be Big Winners, it had yet to open to oil tanker traffic. 

But Louis is preparing for that eventuality by searching for ETFs that will benefit when the Strait opens. Specifically, he's searching to invest in Asian countries that receive a large portion of their crude through the Strait.

These are his picks for ETFs ranked by the highest potential gain:

  1. iShares MSCI China ETF (MCHI). China's dependency is 40-45%. Lower energy costs could increase profits of industrials, consumer, property, and EV sectors. Cheap valuations give the most room for multiple expansion.
  2. iShares MSCI India ETF (INDA). India has unmatched growth momentum in this group and has 42% oil dependency. Cheaper fuel cuts subsidies and helps the profits of consumption and manufacturing.
  3. iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY).  South Korea has a highly energy-intensive economy with semis, autos, and petrochemicals industries dominating. The stocks were already punished hardest by the oil spike, so this could lead to a sharp rebound.
  4. iShares MSCI Japan ETF (EWJ).  Japan is very dependent on this crucial oil supply, with a dependency of 73%. Exports could be more competitive as input costs decline.
  5. iShares MSCI Taiwan ETF (EWT).  This tech-heavy supply chain could rally, but to a lesser degree, potentially as the valuations are more stretched.

Alternatively, Louis says you could simply buy the iShares MSCI All Country Asia ex Japan ETF (AAXJ). It's heavily exposed to China, Taiwan, South Korea, and India.

You can buy a basket of individual Asian ETFs or simply AAXJ. Either way, when the Strait of Hormuz opens fully, an investment in these countries should work well. However, Louis does caution that this is a high-risk trade. There's a war going on, and the one question Louis can't answer is when it will be clear sailing on the Strait of Hormuz.

Ed Ponsi Asks Why Lyft Trades at a Discount to UBER

Once in a while, I ask the questions. Last week, when TipRanks' own Julie Gillespie and I were discussing Pro contributor Stephen Sarge Guilfoyle's pick of Uber on our weekly video, I noticed that Lyft (LYFT) seemed undervalued. So, I went to Ed Ponsi, who's great at combining analyses from stock charts and fundamentals, and asked, "Ed, what's up with Lyft?"

In How Lyft Could Get a Lift as Takeover Target, Ed gives us the lowdown on why this company could be on the acquisition radar.

Let's start with the macro picture. AI is hot. And AI-powered autonomous cars are hot, too. You might say that AI is putting the "auto" in automobile.

NVIDIA's (NVDA) Jensen Huang said it better. He said that now is the ChatGPT moment for self-driving cars. We're at an inflection point where the technology is becoming proven, and the market is ready.

Additionally, while Uber (UBER) is partnering with NVIDIA to launch a robotaxi fleet to rival Tesla (TSLA), Lyft has been partnering with May Autonomy, running a test in Atlanta and Nashville, with a new test coming to Dallas later this year.

Uber is the big name in the space. It has a market cap of $150 billion, around 30 times greater than Lyft's $5.3 billion. And while Uber's customer base is larger, its 200 million users are not 30 times greater than Lyft's 25 million. It's only 8 times more.

So, why is Uber down 9% in 2026 while Lyft is off 30%?

Earnings. In the most recent quarter, Lyft reported revenues of $1.59 billion, nearly 10% below the $1.75 billion expected by analysts. And this disappointment wasn't the first time. Lyft has missed expectations in each of the last five quarters.

But still, when you buy shares in Uber, you're paying 17x trailing earnings. You can buy Lyft for just 3x.
data.author: Jason Meshnick, The Street Pro

data.title: McDonald's (MCD) Set to Outperform as Affordability Gap Narrows
data.description:
The "affordability gap" that defined the post-pandemic era is finally being bridged, though perhaps not by the players we expected. While both consumers and investors worry about the stickiness of service-sector inflation, McDonald's (MCD) has decided to stop playing defense and start reclaiming the "value" crown it nearly lost a few years ago. Despite the noise about rising labor costs, I remain bullish on MCD. It's not really about buying a burger chain, but about a high-volume, tech-enabled infrastructure play that is currently outmaneuvering every rival in sight.

data.title: Micron Stock (MU) Slides as Rival SK Hynix Eyes a U.S. Listing
data.description:
Micron (MU) stock dipped on Tuesday alongside news that rival memory and storage company SK Hynix (HXSCL) is seeking additional funding. According to recent reports, SK Hynix wants to raise $10 billion from a listing in the U.S. This would help the company with its plan to purchase $8 billion of extreme ultraviolet equipment from ASML Holding (ASML) by the end of 2027.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

If SK Hynix pursues a U.S. listing for its shares, it could be a negative catalyst for Micron stock. As it stands now, investors don't have many options for investing in SK Hynix. The company's stock is only available over-the-counter or through the CSOP SK Hynix Daily 2X Leveraged Product ETF (HK:7709). However, a U.S. listing would make it more easily accessible to investors, who could trade out their shares of MU stock for SK Hynix stock.

Despite the potential increase in competition for Micron, the company did get a vote of investor confidence yesterday. Four-star Phillip Securities analyst Yik Ban Chong reiterated a Buy rating and increased his price target to $530 from $500, implying a possible 35.83% upside for the shares. Chong pointed to a strong earnings report and increased demand for its advanced high‑bandwidth memory for AI GPUs as reasons to remain bullish on MU stock.

Micron Stock Slips Today

Micron stock was down 2.55% on Tuesday but was still up 38.15% year-to-date. The stock has also rallied 329.34% over the past 12 months. Those year-over-year gains make sense, as the company has seen its shares boosted by the need for computer components amid the AI boom.

MU stock trading activity today was low, with about 4.45 million shares traded, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 35.7 million shares.
data.author: William White
